title: QA Content Repair Plan
description: Sprint 3A (M3.5): structured emission from the QA reviewer for content-level fixes the reviewer cannot apply directly because of the CLAUDE.md §2.4 invariant (only compose_threat_model.py may write threat-model.md). Each action targets a fragment under .fragments/ — applied deterministically by scripts/apply_content_repair.py, then re-rendered via compose. Disjoint from .qa-repair-plan.json (which is for contract violations). The two plans can both fire in the same run.

schema_version.description: Bumped only when the schema breaks backwards compatibility.

generated.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the QA reviewer wrote this plan.

status.description: Aggregate status. `pass` = no actions; `repair_required` = at least one action with a writable fragment target; `manual_review` = actions exist but none has a writable target (rare — typically a checker false-positive).

md_path.description: Absolute path to the threat-model.md the QA reviewer inspected.

output_dir.description: Absolute path to $OUTPUT_DIR. The applier joins relative fragment paths against this.

check.description: QA-reviewer check ID that produced this action (e.g. `2a`, `6`, `7c`, `10`). Surfaces in the applier's log so the user can trace back.

type.description: Coarse category — drives the applier's per-type validation. `heading_rename_cascade` (post-2026-05) is used for §7.X control H4 renames that must propagate to anchor, controls-covered link, and §7.1 overview-table row in one atomic step. `other` is the catch-all for one-off fixes that don't fit the canonical taxonomy.

fragment.description: Path relative to $OUTPUT_DIR (e.g. `.fragments/security-architecture.md`). MUST be one of the writable structural fragments — the applier hard-rejects writes outside `.fragments/`.

rationale.description: 1-2 sentence explanation surfaced in the applier's diff log. Used by maintainers to audit the QA reviewer's judgement on edge cases.

evidence.description: Free-form evidence (e.g. line number in threat-model.md, T-NNN reference). Not used by the applier; preserved for human review.

find.description: Verbatim string to find — must match exactly once in the fragment, else the action is rejected.

anchor.description: Verbatim anchor string after which `content` is inserted (newline-prefixed).

pattern.description: Python re-syntax regex. The applier compiles it as `re.MULTILINE`.

replacement.description: Python re replacement string — backreferences (`\1`, `\g<name>`) supported.

max_substitutions.description: 0 = unlimited. Anything else caps the substitution count to bound blast radius.

op_heading_rename_cascade.description: Rename an H4 control heading and cascade the rename to every mechanical reference in security-architecture.md: the `<a id="<kebab>">` anchor preceding the heading, every `[<old_name>](#<old-kebab>)` markdown link, and `(e.g. <old_name>)` mentions in the §7.1 overview-table 'Main reason' cell. A plain `replace_string` would only touch the heading itself and leave the three cross-references stale. Required for `subcontrol_naming_canonical` / `auth_method_decomposition` repairs.

old_name.description: The current H4 heading text without the `#### N.M.X ` numbering prefix (the applier matches both forms — numbered and bare).

new_name.description: The canonical replacement name. Must match the `method_whitelist` / canonical vocabulary in data/sections-contract.yaml or data/architectural-controls.yaml. `old_name == new_name` is a no-op (idempotent).
